notes.anchor: On the fifth day before the Ides of February
notes.note: Counting inclusively backward from the Ides of February (the 13th), the fifth day before is 9 February.

notes.anchor: cohorts from Picenum under Lentulus and Thermus
notes.note: Philotimus's optimistic report had Picene cohorts rallying to L. Domitius Ahenobarbus under P. Lentulus Spinther the younger and Q. Minucius Thermus; in fact Picenum had already fallen and Domitius would be trapped and captured at Corfinium within weeks.

notes.anchor: these are with us, that is, at the Formian villa
notes.note: Cicero's seaside villa at Formiae on the Campanian coast was a gathering point in Feb 49 for hesitant senators (Manius Lepidus, Lucius Torquatus, the tribune C. Cassius) weighing whether to follow Pompey out of Italy.

notes.anchor: Gaius Cassius, tribune of the plebs
notes.note: The plebeian tribunes held no imperium and no military command, but their persons were sacrosanct and their veto could block magistrates; C. Cassius's presence among the Pompeians lent the cause constitutional cover.
